message:
fix(fp-io): SqlReadOptions::coerce_float default = true matches pandas (br-o0x6 / fd90.41)

Self-review of fd90 commits surfaced a real pandas divergence:
SqlReadOptions::coerce_float defaulted to false via #[derive(Default)],
but pandas defaults coerce_float=True for pd.read_sql,
pd.read_sql_query, and pd.read_sql_table. Per pandas docs:
'coerce_float : bool, default True. Attempts to convert values of
non-string, non-numeric objects (like decimal.Decimal) to floating
point.'

Replace #[derive(Default)] with a manual impl Default for
SqlReadOptions that produces coerce_float=true and the natural
empty/None values for the other fields. Existing call sites either
pass an explicit value or use ..Default::default() spread, so the
default flip only affects ::default()-constructed instances that
didn't explicitly opt in.

Update sql_read_coerce_float_promotes_decimal_like_text_columns to
reflect the new default (read_sql now coerces by default; an
explicit coerce_float=false opts out and preserves Utf8). Test now
covers all three cases: bare default coerces, opt-out preserves
Utf8, explicit opt-in coerces (round-trip parity).

Add sql_read_options_default_coerce_float_matches_pandas smoke test
asserting opts.coerce_float == true plus all other fields = None.

409 fp-io tests pass; clippy -D warnings clean; ubs Critical=0.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
